name: ETH Failed Breakdown Hold v1
thesis: ETH 4h can reward failed breakdown reclaims that hold instead of instantly reverting. The edge should come from reclaim plus hold persistence, then scaling out as follow-through proves itself.
edge_mechanism: failed breakdown reclaim followed by hold persistence
invalidation_condition: Abort if hold persistence cannot generate enough participation, if filters conflict structurally, or if post-cost edge does not survive walk-forward testing.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: False downside excursions that quickly regain value and then continue as trapped sellers unwind.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop or transition, failed breakdowns often reverse harder than clean breakouts because positioning is wrong-footed and reacceptance forces covering.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is slow enough to show true reclaim-and-hold behavior while still allowing multiple tests over the sample.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If breakdowns do not recover quickly or ADX remains structurally too high, the reclaim signal may be late or absent.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is low but conceptually valid, widen reclaim distance and shorten holding window before adding more indicators.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and low-to-mid ADX are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reclaim threshold and simple qualifier stack

long: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
short: Trail remaining 25% by 0.7 * ATR_14 after first partial
